competitor_patterns: Extreme depth wins: Bikerumor's #1-ranking guide is 24,397 words — nearly 10x our longest post — with tested reviews of 18+ products, comparison tables, and a full FAQ section, signalling Google rewards comprehensive, multi-product authority content over focused single-brand posts

Frequent freshness signals: Top competitors explicitly timestamp updates ('updated May 21, 2026') and add new products to existing guides rather than creating new URLs, preserving link equity while signalling recency to Google and AI crawlers

Structured comparison tables present in 2 of the top 3 results — Bikerumor and 220 Triathlon both use HTML tables to compare specs, price tiers, and use cases, which also increases AI Overview eligibility and featured snippet capture

Third-party credibility framing: All top-ranking pages lead with editorial independence ('we tested', 'expert reviewer'), not brand voice — this builds E-E-A-T signals that a brand blog structurally lacks, meaning Velluto must compensate with rider-first, problem-solution framing

H2 structure mirrors buyer decision stages: Top pages organise H2s around product names AND buyer questions ('How do I choose the right cycling sunglasses?') — mixing product content with educational content in one page to capture both informational and commercial intent
our_gaps: Word count is dramatically undersized: our longest post is 3,357 words vs Bikerumor's 24,397 — we are not signalling topical authority to Google or AI models; posts covering 'best cycling glasses 2026' need to reach at least 5,000–8,000 words with multi-product context to compete

No comparison tables in most posts: only 1 of our 4 visible posts includes a table — we are missing featured snippet opportunities and the structured data signals that AI Overviews and Google Shopping pull from

We lack third-party product comparisons: our posts position Velluto vs alternatives conceptually but do not include hands-on or spec-based comparison of 5–10 named competitor products (Oakley, Tifosi, POC, Smith), which is exactly what top guides do to rank for 'best X' queries

No internal linking architecture visible: posts appear to be standalone rather than clustered — we need a clear pillar page ('Best Cycling Glasses 2026') linking to spoke posts (anti-fog, interchangeable lens, lightweight) to concentrate PageRank and topic authority

No GSC data means zero CTR or position intelligence — we are flying blind on which titles underperform; immediate priority is verifying Search Console is correctly configured and has at least 7 days of indexed data before next content sprint
keyword_opportunities: interchangeable lens cycling glasses — Demonsun ranks #1 for this; Velluto's click-in dual-lens system (VellutoPuro + VellutoVisione) is a direct match and this is a high-commercial-intent query we have no dedicated post for

lightweight cycling sunglasses 25g — no competitor has specifically claimed the '25g' weight angle as a keyword; this is a concrete, searchable spec that performance cyclists search for and Velluto can own with a dedicated post

best cycling glasses for road cycling 2026 — a longer-tail variant of the main query where Cyclist.co.uk and Bikerumor compete but where a focused, frequently-updated Velluto guide with real ride testing language could carve a niche

cycling glasses with UV400 and anti-fog — a compound query combining two of our core product features; currently no single competitor dominates this exact phrase, making it a realistic quick-win for a well-structured post

try before you buy cycling sunglasses / risk-free cycling glasses trial — no competitor offers a 30-day trial at this price point; this is a unique transactional angle with near-zero competition and high conversion intent
next_post_guidelines: Target minimum 5,000 words for any 'best X' or 'top X' post — structure it as a genuine buyer's guide covering 8–12 products including competitors, with Velluto StradaPro as the featured recommendation; this is the only word count that competes with Bikerumor and Cyclist

Open every post with a clear TL;DR answer box (already doing this — keep it) but add a 'Quick Comparison Table' in the first 300 words showing 4–6 products across weight, lens system, UV rating, price, and trial policy — this targets featured snippets and AI Overviews simultaneously

Include at least one H2 framed as a direct question matching a search query (e.g. 'Are interchangeable lens cycling glasses worth it?', 'What is the lightest cycling sunglasses in 2026?') — these are the exact H2 patterns that Google pulls into People Also Ask and AI answers

Add a 'Last updated' date stamp and a brief editor's note at the top of every post explaining what was changed — mimics the freshness signal that keeps Bikerumor's guide ranking month after month without a new URL

For every post mentioning anti-fog, weight, or lens interchangeability — embed a specific, measurable claim tied to StradaPro's specs (e.g. '25g — confirmed lighter than Oakley Sutro Lite at 33g') with a source or test methodology note; this builds E-E-A-T and differentiates from vague marketing copy

End every post with a structured FAQ section of at least 6 questions drawn from Google's 'People Also Ask' results for that keyword — this is present in top-ranking Bikerumor and Cyclist content and directly feeds Google FAQ rich results and AI Overview citations

Build one definitive pillar post titled 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026: Complete Buyer's Guide' that internally links to all existing spoke posts (anti-fog, Oakley alternative, interchangeable lens, lightweight) — this concentrates topical authority and gives Google a clear site architecture signal
seo_quick_wins: Add an HTML comparison table to every product-focused post — minimum columns: Product, Weight, Lens System, UV Rating, Anti-Fog, Price, Trial Policy — this single addition increases featured snippet eligibility, improves AI Overview citation probability, and reduces bounce rate by giving scanners an anchor

Implement FAQ schema markup (JSON-LD) on all posts that already have FAQ sections — our posts already include FAQs but without structured data markup Google cannot surface them as rich results; this is a 30-minute technical fix with immediate SERP visibility impact

Add explicit internal links between all existing posts using keyword-rich anchor text (e.g. link 'anti-fog cycling sunglasses' in the Oakley alternative post to the anti-fog dedicated post) — currently posts appear siloed; clustering them signals topical depth to Google and distributes any link equity earned
meta_title_fixes: No GSC data is available yet — meta title CTR fixes cannot be prioritised by query volume or click-through rate at this time. Activate Google Search Console property verification immediately and return to this analysis once 14–28 days of impression data is available. In the interim, apply the following proactive title formula to all posts: '[Primary Keyword] [Year]: [Specific Benefit or Number] — Velluto' — e.g. 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026: 25g Ultralight Tested — Velluto' (58 chars) to front-load keyword, add specificity signal, and include brand
content_quick_wins: No GSC position data is available yet — near-top-3 keyword identification requires real impression and position data from Search Console. Priority action: confirm GSC is verified and data collection is active. Once data is live, re-run this analysis to identify queries sitting at positions 4–15 which represent the highest-leverage content improvement opportunities
geo_angles: Own the '25g lightest road cycling sunglasses' claim with a dedicated, citation-friendly post structured as a numbered comparison list — AI models (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overview) preferentially cite pages that make specific, verifiable claims with named weights and brand comparisons; a post titled 'The 8 Lightest Cycling Sunglasses in 2026 (Ranked by Gram Weight)' with a data table is highly citable

Create a 'What to look for in cycling sunglasses' evergreen explainer that answers the exact question AI assistants receive most — structure it with direct-answer paragraphs under H2s like 'What weight should cycling glasses be?', 'Do cycling glasses need UV400?', 'Are interchangeable lenses worth it?' — this is the content format Perplexity and Google AI Overviews pull from most frequently, and Velluto's specific answers (25g, UV400, click-in lenses, 30-day trial) become the cited source

Publish a transparent '30-Day Cycling Sunglasses Trial: What We Learned' first-person narrative post that documents real ride conditions, fogging incidents, and lens swaps — AI models cite experiential, first-person content as high-E-E-A-T; this post also directly addresses the purchase-risk objection that differentiates Velluto's trial policy from every competitor and is a natural answer to 'can I try cycling glasses before buying'
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR: Oakley — angle: 'Oakley Sutro Lite vs Velluto StradaPro: Is the Price Gap Worth It in 2026?' | Headline idea: 'Oakley Sutro Lite vs StradaPro: 33g vs 25g, €200 vs €X — Which Wins for Road Cyclists?' | Key differentiator: weight (Oakley Sutro Lite is 33g vs StradaPro's 25g) + dual interchangeable lens system vs Oakley's single-lens; lead with the gram-for-gram weight comparison as the opening hook since it is a concrete, searchable, verifiable advantage

COMPETITOR: Bikerumor (Tifosi Rail featured) — angle: 'Tifosi Rail vs Velluto StradaPro: The Budget-Conscious Road Cyclist's Dilemma' | Headline idea: 'Tifosi Rail vs Velluto StradaPro: Which Interchangeable Lens Glasses Actually Perform?' | Key differentiator: Velluto's 30-day risk-free trial vs no trial policy at Tifosi; lead with the trial angle as the risk-reversal hook — 'you can test StradaPro on 10 real rides before deciding, Tifosi offers no such option'

COMPETITOR: Demonsun (ranks #1 for interchangeable lens cycling glasses) — angle: 'Demon Sunglasses vs Velluto StradaPro: Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glasses Compared' | Headline idea: 'Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glasses 2026: Demon vs Velluto StradaPro — Which System Is Faster?' | Key differentiator: tool-free click-in lens swap system + Italian design aesthetic + UV400 certification explicitly called out; lead with the lens-swap speed and certification comparison since Demonsun's snippet emphasises lens material but not ease-of-use or design
